name: EnergyCAP
description: An energy and utility management platform used to track building consumption, spending, billing quality, and sustainability performance over time.
whyUseIt: Energy performance has become a core part of modern property management. Platforms like EnergyCAP help teams move from utility invoices and rough estimates to structured tracking that supports cost control, ESG reporting, and operational improvement.
aiCapabilities: AI can identify unusual consumption patterns, highlight billing anomalies, and generate short summaries of where usage is moving and why it may matter.
workflowStage: Utility tracking, energy analysis, and ESG reporting
propertyManagerWorkflow: Property managers review utility movement, validate whether consumption changes match operations, and use the outputs in sustainability and owner reporting conversations.
managerExpects: Visible trends, credible data, and practical next steps rather than a sustainability dashboard that no one acts on.
aiWorkflowImpact: AI can help identify which anomalies deserve immediate review and turn meter or invoice data into plain-language action points.
demoDescription: Compare two quarters of building energy use, identify what moved, and recommend whether the issue looks operational, seasonal, or worth deeper engineering review.

vocabulary.definition: The starting reference point used to compare future energy or emissions performance.
The amount of energy or utility resource used over a given period.
An unexpected change in usage or cost that falls outside normal behavior.

beginnerMistakes: Assuming every usage increase is bad without checking occupancy or weather
Ignoring bad data quality and building reports on weak inputs
Reporting utility numbers without suggesting an operational response
